programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow).(n)

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott.(n)

As they uncover the late advocate's unusual hobbies, they gradually learn exactly what happened. Anita Dobson, Jenny Agutter and Keith Barron guest star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An armed police raid at a terraced house in North London sees two suspected terrorists killed, along with a police officer. Nikki doubts the officers' accounts of events after carrying out the post mortems as the forensic evidence just doesn't add up. The case then takes an unexpected turn when it transpires one of the surviving policemen is having an affair with his dead colleague's wife.(n)

It turns out she was a popular member of staff, a charismatic performer and great with the guests - but her mother's reaction to her death makes the detectives suspect there is more to her family situation than meets the eye. And when her colleagues' alibis for the night of the murder don't quite stack up, a different picture of the victim - and the camp - comes to light. programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey are suspected of carrying out a spate of burglaries in Chigwell and snooty man-eater Dorien sees her chance to get rid of the neighbourhood's low-life element once and for all by not speaking up in their defence. With Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph.(n)

programme.title: Waiting for God

They also sample a toffee that is actually a barley sugar, visit Agatha Christie's hide-out hotel and enjoy a slap-up dinner - with Yorkshire pudding for desert.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review.(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Singing for Britain
programme.desc: Gareth Malone sets out to bring the nation together through music during the Coronavirus lockdown, by getting people from all walks of life to perform a specially composed song. Gareth employs technology to bring the voices of performers from across the country together online even if social distancing makes large gatherings impossible. The final episode features the country's most vulnerable people, who have contributed to the project while self-shielding, and discovers their personal stories of finding hope during isolation.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the four remaining contestants take part in the semi final, creating garments inspired by silver screen. First, the semi-finalists must follow a pattern like none they have encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a dress inspired by Marilyn Monroe's in The Seven Year Itch. In the Transformation Challenge, the sewers have to make a sci-fi inspired outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a made to measure flapper dress for the final task of the semi final.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George demonstrates how to detect and deal with garden pests and Kirsty Wilson creates an indoor succulent tower.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: A look inside a bingo hall in the heart of West Belfast, meeting some of the regulars who have known each other for many years, through thick and thin. The programme follows bingo members including serenely composed Eilish, who was taught how to play by her grandmother when she was in her teens, and hard-working Margaret, who juggles her bingo alongside work, a college course, school pick-ups and supporting her husband as he deals with health problems.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a new quest as he travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. Beginning in Hong Kong, the former politician investigates how Britain won the island and Kowloon from China after two mid-19th century wars over the trade in opium. He also boards the island's most famous funicular to the Peak and visits the state-of-the art West Kowloon station with its high-speed link to China - the largest underground high-speed station in the world.(n)

programme.title: Into the Wild with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: The film-maker reveals the extent of Scotland's wildlife, taking well-known faces on an adventure in which they observe some of the UK's most iconic species. In the first programme Gordon is joined by Alastair Campbell for a trip to the Western Isles, including the Isle of Mull, where Gordon grew up, and Tiree, where Alastair spent much of his childhood. The pair go in search of elusive otters and discover an unconventional way of attracting seals, while also getting up close with sea eagles and puffins.(n)

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: Drama about the political movement to pass the Equal Rights Amendment in the US. In the first episode, Phyllis Schlafly - a conservative advocate for Barry Goldwater and perennial candidate for Congress - refrains from running again amid a slew of harassment by her male colleagues and lack of support by her husband Fred. Instead, she sets her sights on a new campaign - fighting the bipartisan support of the Equal Rights Amendment. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks.(n)

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: Gloria Steinem channels her reputation as editor-in-chief of Ms Magazine to lead the fight for women's rights as co-founder of the National Women's Political Caucus - including abortion and equal pay. Meanwhile, Phyllis Schlafly uses her grassroots campaign to stop ERA ratification in her home state of Illinois. Drama about the political movement to pass the Equal Rights Amendment in the US. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul visits Coll, where he meets crofter Angus Kennedy, and also visits the owner of Breachacha Castle, Nicolas Maclean Bristol.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alex Brooker: Disability and Me
programme.desc: The Last Leg presenter confronts what disability really means in Britain today. Alex is reunited with a childhood friend who has spina bifida, who challenges him to consider the impact of making jokes about his own disability. He also pays a visit to a bespoke disability helpline, meets members of Arsenal's disabled supporters club, and talks to Paralympic swimmer Susie Rodgers about her life.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including an Essex pawnbroker who has put off his wedding three times because he has lost confidence in his looks and a biker chick from Newport Pagnell, who rocks up wanting a funked-up feminine look. The experts also get their hands on a stay-at-home mum who wants her fashion mojo back after battling a brain condition and a lifelong military man who would like a smart casual look perfect for a single gentleman in his sixties. Plus, advice for viewers on what jeans work best for different body shapes.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades tells the story of how mid 20th-century furniture continues to influence the modern-day home by revealing the history behind three classic pieces repaired in the barn.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown looks back at some of the show's greatest properties in Cornwall. He also revisits the stories that reflect the Cornish countryside and coast in all its glory.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and Ellie Harrison reflect on an unprecedented year and nature's power to help people cope with such challenging times. Wildlife sound recordist Gary Moore searches for a 'lost' dawn chorus on the Knepp Estate in West Sussex, where numbers of rare songbirds like the nightingale are beginning to recover. Gillian Burke explains how she has been helping with a survey of one of Cornwall's much-loved mammals, the grey seal.(n)

programme.title: Into the Wild with Gordon Buchanan
programme.desc: The film-maker and Ed Byrne are in Essex where they go in search of urban foxes and badgers, and discover that an old disused aerodrome provides the perfect habitat for all sorts of creatures. In Epping Forest they search for fallow deer, and also find an unlikely inhabitant of the Essex countryside - the ring necked parakeet.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen arrive in Birmingham on the latest leg of their culinary journey. They take in the sights and sounds of the Bull Ring market, where they feast on the finest dim sum, take a canal trip through the heart of the city - leading to an impromptu ballet lesson for Mason - and head to the famous Balti Triangle, where they help to create a special Irish curry.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from two classic finals at the All England Club - Serena Williams v Agnieszka Radwanska in 2012 and Pete Sampras v Pat Rafter at 2000.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People in Weston-super-Mare bring their valuables along to the Grand Pier for valuation by experts Jonathan Pratt and Thomas Plant. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers more about the seaside pier itself.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson features some of the most memorable barn conversions featured on Escape to the Country, once a staple of the rural landscape, but now refreshed as stylish homes.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the second day of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The sides also met last year, when the Windies recorded their first series victory over England since 2009, though they did have the benefit of home advantage on that occasion. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig quizzes the panel on a few revolutionary ideas, with Alan Davies, Gyles Brandreth, Susan Calman and Jessica Fostekew going round the houses trying to come up with a decent answer.(n)

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Return of the comedy drama about a family with a learning disabled daughter. Rosie starts to say 'mama', raising hopes that her communication will finally take off. Emily feels totally outshone by Rosie's amazing schoolteacher and concentrates on developing her daughter's sign-language, while Simon worries that the other parents of special needs children look down on him. Things come to a head at the school sports day. Starring David Tennant, Jessica Hynes and Miley Locke.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The spoof documentary about a group of centuries-old vampires sharing a home in Staten Island continues. Laszlo and Nadja are reunited with their old friend Simon the Devious, who is down on his luck and living in the sewers. They offer him shelter at the house - but it soon becomes clear that Simon is out for himself. Meanwhile, Nandor discovers the truth about Guillermo and Colin Robinson takes trolling to the nth degree by sucking the life force from people in internet chat rooms.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nandor's old familiar from the 1970s returns to Staten Island, Guillermo leaves to serve a new master and Nadja and Lazslo revive their musical act. Comedy, starring Kayvan Novak, Harvey Guillen, Matt Berry and Natasia Demetriou, with a guest appearance from Jack O'Connell.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates soft purple sky and tall trees that almost seem to float in the hazy foothills of a distant, blue-grey mountain.(n)

programme.title: Pavlopetri - The City Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Archaeologists use submersible robots and computer simulations to investigate the submerged city of Pavlopetri, which lies off the southern coast of the Greek mainland. Originally rediscovered in 1967, it is believed to be the oldest underwater city in the world. Philip Glenister narrates this documentary following Jon Henderson of the University of Nottingham as he leads a team of experts hoping to discover what life was like for the citizens of Pavlopetri 3,500 years ago.(n)

programme.title: Black Nurses: The Women Who Saved the NHS
programme.desc: The story of the thousands of Caribbean and African women who answered the call 70 years ago to come to the UK and help build the National Health Service. Told by the women themselves, the documentary reveals the price they paid in leaving behind their families, and examines their struggle to overcome racism and their fight for career progression, as well as their cultural impact here and abroad.(n)

programme.title: The Long Song
programme.desc: Robert is increasingly angry and frustrated by the workers' refusal to follow his orders, so he takes violent revenge on them - much to the shock and horror of July. With the crop starting to spoil and Amity in ruins, his sanity begins to unravel - with possibly devastating consequences for those closest to him. Drama, starring Tamara Lawrance and Jack Lowden.(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob presents a profile of the novelist Andrea Levy, charting her journey to become one of the country's best loved contemporary novelists. He looks back at her best-selling novel, Small Island, which captured the imagination of readers around the world, and her most recent novel, The Long Song, has recently been adapted by the BBC, featuring an all-star cast.(n)

programme.title: Being Beethoven
programme.desc: A profile of Ludwig van Beethoven to mark the 250th anniversary of his birth, beginning by exploring his childhood, and his subsequent rise to fame as a piano virtuoso, then composer, in late 18th-century Vienna. Featuring Mark Padmore singing one of Beethoven's early lieder Adelaide, and conductor Marin Alsop talking about the premiere of the Ninth Symphony.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates soft purple sky and tall trees that almost seem to float in the hazy foothills of a distant, blue-grey mountain.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument.(n)

programme.title: Black Nurses: The Women Who Saved the NHS
programme.desc: The story of the thousands of Caribbean and African women who answered the call 70 years ago to come to the UK and help build the National Health Service. Told by the women themselves, the documentary reveals the price they paid in leaving behind their families, and examines their struggle to overcome racism and their fight for career progression, as well as their cultural impact here and abroad.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A cabin finds a peaceful resting place in a warm setting of lush, lakeside greenery in this oval-shaped painting by Bob Ross.(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in China's elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The host explores how the idea of a national character changed during the Victorian era, as suffering in silence came to be regarded as a noble trait - and even a form of civic duty. He visits Ardingly College in West Sussex, the boarding school where he was educated, to consider the benefits and drawbacks of an institutionalised approach to character-building, and talks to MP Rory Stewart, who discusses the emotional demands he faced as a diplomat in Iraq. Finally, he visits the First World War battlefields of the Somme, the place where he believes the flaws in the Victorian value system were brutally exposed.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary that looks at the tough reality of running for politics in a country riven by corruption and tribal factionalism, through the story of Boniface 'Softie' Mwangi.(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Less than 100 years ago, the British ruled a quarter of the planet, and people were proud to call themselves imperialists. But now, to many, that seems like a badge of shame. Here, David Olusoga examines how the British Empire has been portrayed on British TV over the past 70 years, drawing on clips from documentary series Timewatch as well as other gems from the archive.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star.(n)

programme.desc: A cabin finds a peaceful resting place in a warm setting of lush, lakeside greenery in this oval-shaped painting by Bob Ross.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a serene lakeside setting accented with an abundance of his trademark trees.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: The death of Tess's father results in her family being forced out of their house, and she again finds herself at the mercy of Alec, who offers the Durbeyfields a home and support. Angel returns from Brazil and tracks down his wife, but his visit drives Tess to despair, and she lashes out at her benefactor Alec before spending one last night with Angel. Starring Gemma Arterton.(n)

programme.title: Borg vs McEnroe (2017)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1980, acclaimed Swedish tennis player Bjorn Borg is training for an unprecedented fifth consecutive Wimbledon men's singles title, with only one thing standing in his way - the brash and abrasive young American John McEnroe. A rivalry develops between the pair, culminating in one of the greatest matches of all time in that year's final. Biopic, starring Sverrir Gudnason, Shia LaBeouf and Stellan Skarsgard.(n)

programme.title: The History Boys (2006)
programme.desc: Eight unruly grammar school boys preparing to take their Oxbridge entrance exams are exposed to the contrasting teaching styles of two very different schoolmasters - the intellectually enthusiastic Hector, who seeks to instil a love of knowledge for its own sake, and the more cynical new arrival Irwin, who intends to equip them with the specific information they need to get ahead. Comedy drama adapted from Alan Bennett's play, with Richard Griffiths, Stephen Campbell Moore and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch

Matilda's in charge of the party food: build your own spicy sausage pitta bread sandwiches with zingy yoghurt sauce and popcorn cakes. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Maxine Peake performs the writer's monologue Miss Fozzard Finds Her Feet - the tale of a woman whose chiropodist of many years drops the bombshell that he is moving to Scarborough. He suggests Mr Dunderdale, who's retired but likes to keep his hand in. She is also looking after her brother Bernard, who is recovering from a stroke, and she's been instructed to throw language at him to help his recovery. And so, on her return, she switches off the snooker and tells him about Mr Dunderdale and her pre-fungal condition.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a concertina that survived combat in the First World War, a vintage jigsaw, and a cocktail table with a surprising secret compartment. Plus, soft-toy restoration duo Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are called upon to rescue a much-loved toy dog, made by German manufacturer Steiff, which its owner Anita has treasured ever since it was given to her by her grandparents as a young girl.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Actor Phil Daniels, drag artist Baga Chipz, Olympic hockey gold medallist and sports presenter Sam Quek, conductor for the Kingdom Choir Karen Gibson and TV presenter Dominic Littlewood compete in the kitchen. For their first challenge, they head to the MasterChef Market - stocked full of the best quality produce from across the world including meat, fish, fruit, vegetables, nuts, pulses, herbs and spices. The challenge is to invent and then cook one dish to show the judges they have potential. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: In the final show of this special compilation series, Michael looks back at more unforgettable moments from the first five seasons of his Big Show, which began in 2016. There's more amazing and hilarious Send to Alls from Baby Spice Emma Bunton and football legend, pundit and ex-Strictly star Robbie Savage. The Princess of Pop herself Kylie Minogue has a big surprise for some karaoke-singing audience members in Kylie-oake, and Michael turns one audience member's grandpa into a social media star.(n)

The challenge is to invent and then cook one dish to show the judges they have potential. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: A portrait of caravan life on the Ards Peninsula, telling the stories of those who leave their problems behind in the city and head to the seaside. In a revealing depiction of working-class Protestant life, this documentary scratches beneath the stereotype of the affectionately nicknamed Shankill by the Sea to understand what really draws people to holiday here.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the midweek Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Liverpool at Anfield and Manchester City v Newcastle United at St James' Park, where the top two will have been expecting to each take another three points. Plus, Sheffield United v Wolverhampton Wanderers, West Ham United v Burnley, Arsenal v Leicester City, Crystal Palace v Chelsea and Watford v Norwich City.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: In the final show of this special compilation series, Michael looks back at more unforgettable moments from the first five seasons of his Big Show, which began in 2016. There's more amazing and hilarious Send to Alls from Baby Spice Emma Bunton and football legend, pundit and ex-Strictly star Robbie Savage. The Princess of Pop herself Kylie Minogue has a big surprise for some karaoke-singing audience members in Kylie-oake, and Michael turns one audience member's grandpa into a social media star.(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: How dozens of drivers have lost money to a company that sold their car for them, but did not pass on the cash. Plus, online retailers that offer a decidedly poor service.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Glasgow to look round a cottage flat, as well as another cottage in Gnosall, Staffordshire. Dion Dublin explores a one-bedrom flat in Teddington, London.(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shop owner risks her life to save a colleague from an armed robber wielding an axe. Two teenagers steal a life-size ornamental pig.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and Danny Sebastian help two teams in Brackley, Northamptonshire. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp.(n)

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Leonie and Claudia intervene in Sabine's situation. It seems as if Kam is destined never to take his exam.(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple with a £900,000 budget find a home in the Worcestershire countryside, so they can be closer to loved ones. Jonnie also visits Droitwich Spa.(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Leicestershire to pitch ideas to celebrate the buyers' dog. But only two will go on to realise their artworks, before the buyers choose their favourite.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades delves deeper into the stories of three previous fixes to show how immigration has shaped the UK, remembering the restoration of a pump organ from the Caribbean, a barber's pole with Cypriot links and an Iraqi samovar.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants are split into pairs and tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour - but there's a catch. They are separated by a wall, meaning the key to the task is communication as one pair recreates John Torode's pan-fried cod wrapped in ham and the other tackles his calf's liver and champ mash. For the second task, the competitors are sent out on their first mass catering challenge, at the McLaren Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Monica Dolan stars in The Shrine, the second of two new monologues written by Alan Bennett to sit alongside 10 of his classic TV scripts. Lorna visits the spot where Clifford's motorbike crashed, hoping for some closure. He had been such a love. But one day she finds someone else has left some flowers on the shrine.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: A busy night shift begins with Chelsey and Dave providing back-up for advanced paramedic Rob at a road traffic accident, where a driver has overturned his car at a roundabout. It is clear he has been drinking, meaning once they have assessed him, he will be answering questions from the police. Eric and Emily are dispatched to an 83-year-old who is in a lot of pain from her back, after which they answer a call from the police to a suspected assault on a 12-year-old, while senior paramedic Tony is called out to a man found slumped in his car in the city centre.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Covid-19 has killed thousands, but now there are fears the pandemic has caused a crisis in cancer care that could mean many thousands more will die prematurely. Reporter Deborah James, who herself has incurable bowel cancer, explores the impact of the focus on coronavirus on cancer treatment. She investigates how the NHS has managed care during lockdown, speaks to experts and analyses new research, and also meets fellow patients to discover what the pandemic has meant for them.(n)

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Spring arrives, and the youngster is very fond of the new lambs.(n)

programme.title: Bruno
programme.desc: The monkey plays with a large green drum and a small yellow one.(n)

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: Maurice an his friends gather to show off their rather unusual talents.(n)

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Magaidh's hens escape, so Shane has no eggs to serve for his new breakfast menu.(n)

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: When Yoko plans a tea party, Louie draws a pigeon to help them carry the invitations.(n)

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The animals learn about the icicles, discovering they have a connection with water.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Tony Kearney reads Cù air a Dhroch Dhealbh, written and illustrated by Emma Dodson and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold is jealous of Danger Mouse's growing collection of medals, which leads him to fall under the spell of Quark's latest money-making scheme.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to set up a space-age satellite and make a magic mirror. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: A zebra must learn about life on the savannah by following in his father's footsteps, the leader of this particular zebra herd. However, the road ahead is hazardous and Ziggy must travel great distances to find food, evade lions and cross crocodile infested rivers. Will he survive, earn his stripes and be promoted to leader of his own zebra squad?(n)

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Observational documentary following young shepherds Archie and Ben, and outdoors enthusiast and head gamekeeper Rory McGillivray as they enjoy North Uist Games day. Meanwhile, in South Uist, Angus and Neil are busy gathering seafood for export to Spain.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Sports clubs, such as one concerned with bicycles and another synchronised swimming.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Na Tiristich (From Tiree with Love)
programme.desc: Tiree is approximately 12 miles long, five miles wide, and has a population of around 650. Cameras reveal what it is like to live on the most westerly island in the Inner Hebrides.(n)

programme.title: An Innocent Man - Sgeulachd Oscar Slater
programme.desc: A look at Glasgow's 'square mile of murder', beginning with an insight into the case of Oscar Slater, widely believed to be the greatest miscarriage of justice in Scottish history.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The 1950s and '60s saw many young women from the islands in Scotland head to the mainland to work in hotels all over the country. With little work on offer at home, for many there was no choice, while others followed older girls to an exciting, new life. This documentary hears the stories of some of those 'hotel girls'.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes.(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Wild swimmer Calum Maclean takes to the water at Dunnet Head, on the north coast of mainland Scotland.(n)

programme.title: Slighe Anndra
programme.desc: Exploring Scottish churches in cities across Europe. Featured locations include London, Lisbon, Paris and Rome.(n)

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service.(n)

programme.title: Lon Le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod and her friends Maya and Anthony bake soda bread. Before they start, they must make sure they have all the ingredients. If not, they prepare a list and go shopping.(n)

programme.title: Su Shiusaidh (Little Suzy's Zoo)
programme.desc: Lulla sings a lullaby to help her friends get to sleep, but it only leaves them more awake.(n)

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack meets Leo and Dara and their cat, Bòtannan.(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: While at the park, Bing spots Pando on his new skateboard and decides he wants a go.(n)

programme.title: Na Floogals
programme.desc: The Floogals find a Hooman fruit called a banana. They learn that it is a yellow, has a very slippery skin, and that the energy derived from it when eaten lasts a long time.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Iain MacRae reads Mo Dhadaidh Iongantach, written and illustrated by Ross Collins and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: When Titch meets a genie and is granted a wish, he is sure that there is only one thing that will make his life better - to be taller. However, the little lad is about to face some bigger problems. Short Gaelic film for younger viewers.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make toys fly using the power of leverage and ensure victory in a game of tug o'war. Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald present.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: The story of a newborn vervet monkey called Viva, who lives with her troop on a golf course in Durban, South Africa.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: John Carmichael investigates the stories behind the whiskies of the Scottish Islands, including the relatively unknown but highly acclaimed Isle of Jura malt.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Observing machines at work, including robots.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Leugh Mi (Book Show)
programme.desc: Anna Macleod talks to Priscilla Scott and Adam Dahmer about books of special interest to them, including Susan Vreeland's Girl in Hyacinth Blue, and Ronald Hutton's The Witch.(n)

programme.title: Cumhachd a' Yoik (The Power of Yoik)
programme.desc: Seumas MacLetchie goes on the journey of a lifetime to meet Ingá-Máret, a Sami yoik artist in Finnish Lapland. In the land of the aurora borealis and Santa trips, he discovers how Ingá-Máret draws on her ancestral tradition of yoiking to protect her reindeer from the threats and impacts of the 21st century.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Despite Pàdruig's return to Camus, Sarah Jane is determined to put the past behind her. However, this desire is challenged when he visits the house with Cailean, which forces her to reveal to Màiri the real reason she's kept this dark secret to herself.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh visit Connemara in the west of Ireland to discover more about traditional music in the area. With them from Scotland are renowned singer Gillebride MacMillan and fiddler Megan Henderson.(n)

programme.title: Mathair
programme.desc: The story of Kathleen Kearney, the Dublin based socialist and republican who was admired for her broad knowledge of literature and history.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Previously unseen musical gems from the series Port, with Julie Fowlis. Calum Stewart joins local musicians Liam Flanagan and Derek Hickey in Limerick to play An Seanduine Crom.(n)

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the Gaelic song About Last Night, sung by Iain R Douglas. Starring Siri Rodnes and Ross Kilgour.(n)

programme.desc: Anne Lorne Gillies reads Bogsa-dhèideagan Ciopair, written and illustrated by Mick Inkpen and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold is jealous of Danger Mouse's growing collection of medals, which leads him to fall under the spell of Quark's latest money-making scheme.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to call room service on a home-made telephone, and the truth about the effects of baked beans. Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald present.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: In the shallows of Hawaii, life is hard. Catching enough food and avoiding bigger sharks isn't easy and only one in 10 baby hammerheads survives to see its first birthday. If she reaches the age of one Hula will have to leave Hawaii, otherwise she will starve. This means an epic journey to find waters teeming with life, thousands of kilometres away. Here, cameras observe as she learns ocean survival skills.(n)

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Observational documentary following young shepherds Archie and Ben, and outdoors enthusiast and head gamekeeper Rory McGillivray as they enjoy North Uist Games day. Meanwhile, in South Uist, Angus and Neil are busy gathering seafood for export to Spain.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Sports clubs, such as one concerned with bicycles and another synchronised swimming.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Ceathrar air a' Chuiltheann (Four on the Cuillin)
programme.desc: Vertigo, fatigue, hunger and the cold threatens the bid of school leavers Rosie, Zach, Mairead and Siobhan to tackle the Cuillin Ridge mountain climb.(n)

programme.title: Mathair
programme.desc: This programme tells the story of the 1981 Hunger Strike through the eyes of a mother, Alice McElwee, who had to suffer the death of her son.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Cork and Limerick in 2015 and 2016. Guests include Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh, who joins Julie Fowlis, Phil Cunningham and Anna-Wendy Stevenson for a version of An Cailín Deas Óg.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes.(n)

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the diversity of the Commonwealth islands of the Queen. From weathered stone huts in the in upland moors to golden Caribbean beaches, the series shows the isles in all their contrast and glory.(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, and swims in the River Truim, which flows north in Badenoch into the Spey.(n)

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Donnie Mackay heads to the Grampian Transport Museum in Alford, for a car show with a difference. Linda MacLeod drives BMW's new baby M car, the M2, considering whether it can put the fun factor back into the German manufacturer's arsenal. Plus, the team members learn what lies in store for them in this year's Ralaidh na Trealaich.(n)

programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features.(n)

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly.(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five members of a LGBT hockey side answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts six more contestants through their paces to see who will have the opportunity to answer 15 questions to win the prize of £1,000,000. Each player has four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Mediterranean with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores an unusual hotel built into the side of a volcano on the Greek island of Santorini, before journeying to north Africa's Mediterranean coast, where he meets movie mogul Tarak Ben Ammar, the man who brought Star Wars to Tunisia. The final port of call on this leg of his trip is on the southern shores of France, where he meets the cowboys of the Camargue and witnesses a spectacular parade at the headquarters of the French Foreign Legion at Aubagne, near Marseille.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: The film-maker investigates how the housing crisis has resulted in some vulnerable families agreeing to be moved by local councils - sometimes many miles away from loved ones - just to keep a roof over their heads. He meets a mother of three whose temporary accommodation across London has ended up being her home for three years so far, and also hears from families in Bradford who say that refusing an offer of accommodation 200 miles away would bring shocking repercussions.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick suggests to Leanne that they should go to a support group for parents of terminally ill children, Tim is shocked to find out that Sally has visited Yasmeen, and Roy apologises for upsetting Evelyn.(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. After a pit stop at Niagara Falls, the Walshes head to Lake Placid for a game of ice hockey, followed by a stay at the Trapp Family Lodge. Brad and Barney take on a frozen rock face, before dog sledding through a winter wonderland in Vermont. Then, the duo continue on their roadtrip to take rally cars for a spin on the ice, before bowling at Moose Alley. To help with the extreme weather, Barney's arranged for some wilderness training, as the boys go lobster fishing off Swan's Island.(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Tiger and Joey go mountain-climbing and soon have a run in with a hiker who takes exception to their seemingly loutish behaviour. The situation worsens when they realise they've forgotten to bring any water. After Pauline's relapse, the Maltbys decide to cut their holiday short and take her home on the evening flight, while romance is in the air at the Solana with both Eddie and Rob landing themselves dates, and a stranger turns up at reception asking for Mateo. Guest starring Angus Deayton.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection filmed by viewers, including a toddler terrified by a clam, a cyclist falling into a filthy canal and a teenager accidentally hitting his dad with a sledgehammer.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The travel industry is in dire straits, with the coronavirus crisis leaving summer holiday plans in disarray for many people. But with the prospect of travel beginning to open up, should holiday-makers be trying to grab a break abroad, or is this the year of the Great British staycation? Helen Skelton investigates.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty asks the questions as the two celebrity captains lead out their families in the final, hoping they and their relatives have what it takes to lift the Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family trophy and take away £25,000 for their chosen charity.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: As life in lockdown continues, the financial journalist presents more advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Manhunt: The Raoul Moat Story
programme.desc: Ten years have passed since Newcastle bodybuilder Raoul Moat went on the run after shooting his ex-girlfriend Samantha Stobbart and her new boyfriend Chris Brown, killing the latter. Nicky Campbell presents this documentary looking back at the real-life drama as it unfolded in July 2010. Archive footage combine with interviews with family members, witnesses and journalists to piece together the tragic events that gripped the nation, and only ending when Moat shot himself dead while surrounded by armed police.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with two people whose lives have been transformed. Kathleen Fraser Jackson was reunited with her birth mother in Canada, and the programme follows her to Jamaica to meet her youngest sister Peta Gay for the first time. The second story has never been shown before and features twins who were separated as babies.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection filmed by viewers, including a toddler terrified by a clam, a cyclist falling into a filthy canal and a teenager accidentally hitting his dad with a sledgehammer.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The travel industry is in dire straits, with the coronavirus crisis leaving summer holiday plans in disarray for many people. But with the prospect of travel beginning to open up, should holiday-makers be trying to grab a break abroad, or is this the year of the Great British staycation? Helen Skelton investigates.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty asks the questions as the two celebrity captains lead out their families in the final, hoping they and their relatives have what it takes to lift the Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family trophy and take away £25,000 for their chosen charity.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: As life in lockdown continues, the financial journalist presents more advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Manhunt: The Raoul Moat Story
programme.desc: Ten years have passed since Newcastle bodybuilder Raoul Moat went on the run after shooting his ex-girlfriend Samantha Stobbart and her new boyfriend Chris Brown, killing the latter. Nicky Campbell presents this documentary looking back at the real-life drama as it unfolded in July 2010. Archive footage combine with interviews with family members, witnesses and journalists to piece together the tragic events that gripped the nation, and only ending when Moat shot himself dead while surrounded by armed police.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with two people whose lives have been transformed. Kathleen Fraser Jackson was reunited with her birth mother in Canada, and the programme follows her to Jamaica to meet her youngest sister Peta Gay for the first time. The second story has never been shown before and features twins who were separated as babies.(n)

programme.title: Bromans
programme.desc: The remaining men compete in the Emperor's Games - the grand final. They and their girlfriends have shed blood, sweat and tears to make it this far, but just one lad will leave Rome a hero and be crowned the Ultimate Broman. In their most brutal battles yet, the lads go to war in the Colosseum in front of a packed crowd. Finally, after weeks of mystery, the identity of the Emperor is revealed. Narrated by Roman Kemp.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The principal of the local school tries to improve test scores by expelling its worst pupils, and Lois is outraged when Chris is the first student to get the chop. Peter's fight with a giant chicken continues - but the pair soon realise they have no idea why they are actually quarrelling.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers his mother had an affair while on holiday in Ireland 40 years previously, and crosses the Atlantic to search for his real father. Meanwhile, Stewie develops a love of punishment.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan reveals his next-door neighbour is gay during a conversation with the man's father, and tries to find a way to make amends for his blunder. Meanwhile, Roger buys alcohol for Steve and his friends.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan forgets to buy grenadine at the grocery store, so an enraged Roger takes revenge by replacing him as chairman of the Homeowners' Association - and starts to abuse his own power. With the neighbourhood's American identity under threat, the spy enlists the help of his children to regain control of the community.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: The cable company receives two courtside tickets for the NBA All-Star basketball game in Los Angeles, and Mr Waterman decides to give them to Cleveland - who gets into trouble at the event for heckling the players. Meanwhile, Rallo befriends a fellow 'little person' at the grocery store.(n)

programme.title: The Almost Impossible Gameshow
programme.desc: Action-packed game show, with commentary from Irish comedy duo the Rubberbandits. Ten contestants are given 50 lives to take on a series of tricky challenges. The aim of the game is to complete five of them without losing all of their lives. Challenges include Tiny Bike, Dizzy Hurdle and Groin Croissant.(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Australia
programme.desc: Excitement in the villa peaks when two new boys enter and Sophie returns with a big announcement. Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a summer of romance compete to pair off in Majorca, in the hope of finding love and winning a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Evil Monkeys
programme.desc: Entertainment game show in which a trio of celebrities are forced to take on a series of challenges on an island ruled by apes. The famous faces making monkeys of themselves are Towie trio Bobby Norris, James Lock and Yazmin Oukhellou.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois campaigns for election as mayor, but is only successful after dumbing down her political views to appeal to the electorate. However, the power of her office soon corrupts her and she begins to accept bribes to buy expensive things for herself. Meanwhile, Peter enjoys all the perks his status as 'first lady' of Quahog brings him.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: An unfortunate experience on a tanning bed leaves Stewie convinced he has skin cancer. Meanwhile, Chris discovers rival paper boy Kyle has stolen one of his customers - so Peter beats him up.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan goes out with some co-workers, but their night gets completely out of control when bodies pile up in the boot of Dick's car. Meanwhile, Roger and Hayley try to find out which of them is better looking. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Scott Grimes.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve asks Stan to join him in a father-son bike tournament, but discovers he has never learnt to ride a bicycle. Meanwhile, Roger hires Hayley to work in a makeshift bar that he has opened in the attic.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna becomes jealous when a slimmer, prettier version of Cleveland's former school friend 'Fatty Patty' turns up and reveals she has a crush on him. However, the newcomer goes crazy and tries to steal Cleveland's 'essence', forcing Donna to intervene.(n)

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as three friends take on celebrities - Mark Francis, Harriet Kemsley, Lloyd Griffith and Snoochie Shy.(n)

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Horror game show, hosted by Reggie Yates, in which two brothers and a sister enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. Along with the classic quizzes, trolley dashes and pastel jumpers, this rebooted incarnation features exciting new games, challenges and some famous faces.(n)

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play her outrageously fun games including You Bet Your Wife, Blindfolded Musical Chairs, Say Whaaat? and Make It Rain.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage, including a cat's accident involving a DVD player, a woman coming to grief on stepping stones and a priest in the wrong place at the wrong time.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts another star-laden edition of the picture-based game show, inviting boxer David Haye, Loose Women's Andrea McLean and former X Factor judge Louis Walsh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. The greater each star performs, the better their chance of securing thousands of pounds for their chosen charity.(n)

programme.desc: Liz has a dangerous encounter, and Kyle begins to spiral. Meanwhile, Max and Michael find themselves at a dead end with the fourth alien.(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Australia
programme.desc: Excitement in the villa peaks when two new boys enter and Sophie returns with a big announcement. Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a summer of romance compete to pair off in Majorca, in the hope of finding love and winning a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Evil Monkeys
programme.desc: Entertainment game show in which a trio of celebrities are forced to take on a series of challenges on an island ruled by apes. The famous faces making monkeys of themselves are Towie trio Bobby Norris, James Lock and Yazmin Oukhellou.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois campaigns for election as mayor, but is only successful after dumbing down her political views to appeal to the electorate. However, the power of her office soon corrupts her and she begins to accept bribes to buy expensive things for herself. Meanwhile, Peter enjoys all the perks his status as 'first lady' of Quahog brings him.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: An unfortunate experience on a tanning bed leaves Stewie convinced he has skin cancer. Meanwhile, Chris discovers rival paper boy Kyle has stolen one of his customers - so Peter beats him up.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan goes out with some co-workers, but their night gets completely out of control when bodies pile up in the boot of Dick's car. Meanwhile, Roger and Hayley try to find out which of them is better looking. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Scott Grimes.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve asks Stan to join him in a father-son bike tournament, but discovers he has never learnt to ride a bicycle. Meanwhile, Roger hires Hayley to work in a makeshift bar that he has opened in the attic.(n)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna becomes jealous when a slimmer, prettier version of Cleveland's former school friend 'Fatty Patty' turns up and reveals she has a crush on him. However, the newcomer goes crazy and tries to steal Cleveland's 'essence', forcing Donna to intervene.(n)

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as three friends take on celebrities - Mark Francis, Harriet Kemsley, Lloyd Griffith and Snoochie Shy.(n)

programme.desc: Horror game show, hosted by Reggie Yates, in which two brothers and a sister enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. programme.desc: Josie is bemused when Vera tells her about seeing Ivy's ghost. Maureen is shocked to discover Maud has proposed to Percy. Mike urges Alma to cater for Stephen.(n)

programme.desc: Maxine arrives late for work after a wild night out. Kevin is annoyed when he has to look after Daniel. Des is surprised when Liz asks him to drink in a different pub to avoid Jim.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank arranges for his baby to be christened in hospital, he and Chris call security when Dave arrives at the hospital, and Kim struggles to bond with her new arrival.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sam spends the night in the police cells, and the Dingles tell him he could be facing a long prison sentence. Dave is still desperate to see the baby.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police leap into action when a labourer at the Kirkbys' farm dies of strychnine poisoning, believing a killer is on the loose. As the finger of suspicion moves from one villager to another, the scene is set for tragedy. Meanwhile, the Ashfordly Rotary Club pensioners head for the seaside town of Whitby - but David's mangy mutt Alfred proves to be something of a liability.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid believes he is in for a fortune when a friend mentions him in his will. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton tries to catch a thief who has been stealing tractors from farms around Aidensfield. programme.title: Midsomer Murders

Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Mary's secret assignation with Lord Gillingham is almost exposed - until Violet saves the day. It seems that the battleaxe may have more reason than most to sympathise with her granddaughter's position when a face from her past reappears, hinting at a hidden past of her own. Meanwhile, Carson is dismissive of Mrs Patmore's latest problem, Branson receives a proposition about the estate's future and the circumstances of nasty valet Green's demise are scrutinised - again.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.desc: Maxine is certain she'll be sacked when Jon takes over the Salon. Vera feels Ivy needs to contact her but doesn't know how to go about it.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Sarah Bunting continues to wage her one-woman war against the Crawleys - before pushing them too far. Violet is too busy getting flustered about Prince Kuragin to notice, and finally confides in Isobel the truth about their shared past. Following her illicit trip to Liverpool with Lord Gillingham, Mary makes a decision about their relationship - but does it have any future? Thomas returns to Downton a shadow of his former self, and Baxter wonders if there's more to his mystery illness than he's letting on. Rose's father Shrimpie (guest star Peter Egan) is also back on the estate - with shocking news.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective discovers his favourite 1970s band are on the verge of a comeback - along with their singer Esme Ford, who has made a shocking reappearance from beyond the grave, having faked her own death 35 years earlier. But when an orphan is found dead on the other side of town, a forensic link leads Lewis and Hathaway to suspect the aged rockers are somehow involved. Joanna Lumley, David Hayman, Simon Callow and Helen Baxendale guest star alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser.(n)

programme.desc: Rita encourages Fiona to try and raise the money to buy the Salon. Steve and Vicky do their best to impress Stephen. Ken is pleased to hear that Denise is moving out of the area.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve is annoyed to discover Mike has won the contract. Jim is furious to find his savings gone. Stephen is taken aback when Alma makes a pass at him in his hotel room.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Cora enjoys the attention Simon Bricker gives her, but is he about to cross a line - and does she want him to? Cora's eldest child, Mary, is also enjoying male company, this time that of Charles Blake, who's about to do her a big favour. Meanwhile, Rose meets a dashing stranger, Branson makes up his mind about Sarah Bunting and Anna is disturbed by a visit from Inspector Vyner.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local female detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones.(n)

programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Lady Mary's secret assignation with Lord Gillingham is almost exposed - until Violet saves the day. It seems that the battleaxe may have more reason than most to sympathise with her granddaughter's position when a face from her past reappears, hinting at a hidden past of her own. Meanwhile, Carson is dismissive of Mrs Patmore's latest problem, Branson receives a proposition about the estate's future and the circumstances of nasty valet Green's demise are scrutinised - again.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)
