programme.desc: Jack tries to save the village from a swarm of ravenous butterflies, but ends up accidentally causing a volcanic eruption.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb gives her expert opinion to British house-hunter Adrian Wilkes, who is searching for a property on the Caribbean island of Grenada, where he hopes to purchase the perfect holiday home for £60,000.(n)

programme.title: D-Day: The Shortest Day
programme.desc: Survivors provide a compelling insight into the D-Day landings, discussing the preparations, their actions, and the consequences of an operation which had such a crucial bearing on the outcome of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: How southerners reacted to the threat of Hitler's V1 flying bombs, which killed 452 people in a week at one point in the campaign. Notoriously inaccurate, the doodlebugs often fell short of their target and hit villages along the south coast from Kent to Dorset, prompting thousands in Chiselhurst to start living in caves.(n)

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra.(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley.(n)

programme.title: A Chorus of Disapproval (1988)
programme.desc: A shy widower gets entangled in romantic intrigue while rehearsing with an amateur theatrical troupe in Scarborough. Comedy based on Alan Ayckbourn's stage play, starring Jeremy Irons, Anthony Hopkins, Prunella Scales, Lionel Jeffries, Jenny Seagrove, Gareth Hunt and Patsy Kensit.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Poplar for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Patsy Emmerson hope to buy a dream holiday home in Gran Canaria for £100,000. They check out a beachside apartment, a country house, a hillside villa and a luxury retreat. Presented by Amanda Lamb.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Ash and Machin must dispose of a bomb in a school - only to find another device close by, forcing them to split up and defuse one each.(n)

programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV
programme.desc: A retrospective review of Bowie's classic album, featuring extensive interviews with Woody Woodmansey and the late Trevor Bolder.(n)

programme.title: The Moodys
programme.desc: The family throws a surprise 40th birthday party for Bridget and it's up to Roger to keep her in the dark. Taking his duties seriously, he organises a day of activities to prevent her from cottoning on, but there's a bigger surprise in store when Roger unveils his present at the gathering.(n)

programme.title: Content Content
programme.desc: Sitcom based in the offices of Content Content, a digital content agency that nobody, including the employees, can pronounce correctly.(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)
